repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Bayard Taylor papers
did.abstract: Correspondence, journals, notebooks, scrapbooks, manuscripts, diaries, clippings, articles, sketches, and autobiographical notes pertaining to Taylor's life as a writer and traveler in North Africa, Scandinavia, Europe, and the Middle East.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Traveler, translator, writer, man of letters, non-resident professor of German literature at Cornell University.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, journals, notebooks, scrapbooks, manuscripts, diaries, clippings, articles, sketches, and autobiographical notes pertaining to Taylor's life as a writer and traveler in North Africa, Scandinavia, Europe, and the Middle East.

Also, a watercolor landscape by Taylor acquired by Dr. Francis Harvey Green and given to William F. Rochow in 1950, with "Gems of Thought," a card with excerpts from Taylor's writings selected by Green.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Bayard Taylor papers, #14-18-1169. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid. 4 MB PDF file.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Not all the material in the collection is covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: Series I. Documents and manuscripts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Loose materials in envelopes--titles mainly taken from envelopes. BT stands for Bayard Taylor. Includes some correspondence.

did.unittitle: BT's connection with the centennial

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of a hymn written by BT for the centennial and sent to Whittier. Correspondence from Jos. R. Hawley, President of the International Exhibition, US Centennial Commission

did.unittitle: Golden wedding masque and additional letters mentioned in Mrs. Taylors letter of June 14, 1905

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains printed catalogue of autograph leeters - a selection from the literary correspondence of BT and many others (June 21, 1905). Nothing appears to be related the a wedding, ets.

did.unittitle: Special letters and notes from the Taylor correspondence
Letters to Bayard Taylor
Document fragments, chiefly from BT

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some fragments are imperfect. Also includes one undated note of Aldrich to Taylor.

did.unittitle: Notes, letters, and invitations to the Taylors for various functions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder labelled "curiosities." Invitations are in French. Also includes phrenological character of Bayard Taylor by Lorenzo Niles Fowler, May 3, 1850.

did.unittitle: Letters and memos about Faust
Faustiana letters and notes
Collections for Goethe in general
Official documents, St. Petersburg Mission

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from W.H. Seward, Secretary of State, and copies of dispatches of B.T. from St. Petersburg

did.unittitle: Only Once

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original papers by various contributors, published for benefit of New York Infirmary for Women and Children.

did.unittitle: A Memorial of Fitz-Greene Halleck
Translations from Bayard Taylor's English works into German
Dedication of the Halleck Monument at Guildford, Connecticut
Clippings, etc.
Oration of Honorable O.P. Morton, address of Major General George G. Meade and dedication ode for National Cemetery at Gettysburg
Printed notices relating to Faust
Appendix to editorial notes for Bayard's poems prepared by Mrs. Bayard Taylor
Biographical sketch for the intended Cambridge edition
A few letters to Bayard Taylor
Small autograph-type album with poems and flower pictures
Scrapbook I
Scrapbook II
Scrapbook III

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings, correspondence, and printed items relating to farewell dinner for B.T.

did.unittitle: Scrapbook IIIa

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings, regrets about not being able to attend farewell dinner for B.T. (1878-04-04), printed items, 1876 Centennial Exposition at Philadelphia, PA.

did.unittitle: Scrapbook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings, correspondence, and printed items regarding Bayard Taylors death.

did.unittitle: Scrapbook V

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bayard Taylor's contributions to newspapers. Does not contain correspondence, but does include some loose newspapers.

did.unittitle: Scrapbook VI

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bayard Taylor's contributions to newspapers and magazines.

did.unittitle: Manuscripts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The package, containing manuscripts on yellow paper, is labelled "The within edition of Bayard Taylor's Completed Poems was prepared by me [presumably Mrs. Taylor] in the years 1895-1896 in the hopes that Houghton's, Mifflin & Co., would publish it. They, however, declined to do so."

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous printed matter, in German and English
Printed page proofs of the play "Prince Deukalion"
Five manuscript and typed pages of autobiographical sketch by Bayard Taylor
Blank Book III

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First few pages appear to have postage accounts of John Taylor. Pages also have childish scribblings and drawings. Also contains some written essays and poems.

did.unittitle: Diary inscribed J.B. Taylor
Wallet type folder containing miscellaneous writings, poems, etc.
Blank Book V
Manuscripts of early prose writings - scraps
Manuscript poems, etc.
The National Ode manuscripts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This consists of 9 pages in B.T.'s handwriting submitted in 1876 for reproduction by facsimile to Jas. R. Osgood & Co., Boston.

did.unittitle: Series II. Journals and Notebooks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diaries were numbered in no particular order. The numbering often continues where the folder numbers leave off for that box, and were not numbered separately from folders.

did.unittitle: Schooldays - notes and sketches
Diary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary for January and February followed by sparse entries for remainder of the year: Arrived in England July, arrived in Heidelburg September, arrived in Frankfurt October.

did.unittitle: Sketch of a ramble through Europe

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Begun on the Atlantic, July 5, 1844. Finished in Florence, December 19, 1845.

did.unittitle: Original poems
Notebook of places visited in Germany, with poems and expenditures
Diary
First drafts of poems
Diary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Begins in Phoenixville, January 21, 1847. Intermittent entries to December 1950, and one entry for June 10, 1854. Included personal thoughts, prayers, and self-analysis.

did.unittitle: Diary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Intermittent entries, with some expenses and miscellaneous notes. United States and Mexico.

did.unittitle: Pencil sketches of various places in Mexico, and poems from 1850
Diary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Travel descriptions, including Albania, Corfu, etc. Also poems, expenditures, and miscellaneous information (undated)

did.unittitle: Diary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Begins in London, followed by Germany (October 5, 1851), ends with Ethiopia.

did.unittitle: Diary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Begins in Ethiopia, ends in Cairo.

did.unittitle: Diary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Begins in Syria, ends in Greece.

did.unittitle: Small notebook containing original poems and some sketches
Diary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Begins in Gibraltar, ends in India, Singapore, and China.

did.unittitle: Journal
Diary
Diary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Norway, Greece, and Poland. Apologies at the end of the diary that "journals for the past seven years have not been more complete."

did.unittitle: Diary
Diary pages
Diary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Trip to Nuremburg, Munich, Majorca, and Spain, April to June 1867. Also a trip to Italy, February 1868.

did.unittitle: Notes from diaries and Journals I
Notes from diaries and Journals II
Notes from diaries and Journals III
Letters, miscellaneous catalogue No. I
Reviews of the translation of Faust
Notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes of books read or bought, translations made, names of poems written by Bayard Taylor.

did.unittitle: Clippings and articles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed and written, some by Bayard Taylor.

did.unittitle: Series III. Incoming correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All letters are originals unless otherwise noted.
